mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.08% indicates extremely accommodative monetary policy. This reduces discount rates on equities and stimulates investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: If the forward 60-day return is negative in more than 50% of rolling windows during periods of FEDFUNDS below 0.1%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 7.5%, it signals severe labor market slack and reduced consumer spending power. This depresses corporate earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 7.5%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of unemployment above 7% and federal funds rate below 0.1% reflects a trapped economy with exhausted monetary policy room. This joint condition signals persistent stagnation and negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: extremely accommodative policy with lingering labor slack
failure_condition: If the signal does not predict negative returns in at least 60% of instances when conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: During a consumer-driven expansion phase, accelerating retail sales growth above 2% month-over-month signals robust demand. This drives corporate revenue gains and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as markets react to leading consumption data.
failure_condition: If positive SPX 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances following RSAFS acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1% signals contraction in the manufacturing sector. This leads to downward revisions in GDP forecasts and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 30% of instances following INDPRO decline > 1%.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly payroll employment gains exceed 200,000, it indicates strong labor demand and rising income. This boosts consumption expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as the market prices in stronger economic growth.
failure_condition: If SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances after strong payrolls.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1,200 thousand units signal strength in residential investment, a key demand component. This leads to multiplier effects across the economy and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as the recovery in housing gains momentum.
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of instances when HOUST exceeds 1,200.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the consumer price index above 0.3% signals rising inflation. This raises expectations of monetary tightening, which depresses equity valuations via higher discount rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following high inflation readings.
